regions.text: Nerves connect the retina to the brain. Impulses travel through these nerves like a million separate telephone lines.
Signals go to the back of the brain, allowing us to see. (Diagram: A human head showing the connection from eye to brain).
Signals travel at the same rate from all parts of the body, even over long distances.
impulse: a signal wave in a nerve. nerve: a chain of cells carrying signals. (Our nerves can get tired).
